todos.lynn-creek-placers.usgsMrds: Mindat page (loc-60622) shows an Other Databases > USGS MRDS link with a record number. Fetch the exact MRDS ID from mindat.org/loc-60622.html and replace null. Until then the property template must NOT render the MRDS link block for Lynn Creek.

items.name: Lynn Creek Placers
items.deposit: The channel extends eastward from the lode mining area for approximately 4 miles. In the upper 2.5 miles the gravels average 25 ft wide and 10 to 28 ft deep, and the pay streak is the first 1.5 ft of alluvium lying on bedrock.
items.workings: Numerous cuts, pits, trenches, and shafts.
items.reserves: Indicated reserves exceed 1 million cubic yards.
items.geology: Gold is probably derived from mineralized shear zones in chert and shale of the Vinini Formation. The recovered gold is very fine, averaging 920 to 960 fineness.

history.event: Channel being worked by the Ura Gold Mines Co.

items.name: Adelaide Mine
items.deposit: Sulfide replacement of marble and calcareous siltstone. Replacement bodies appear to follow transposed bedding (isoclinally folded rock), with north-trending high-angle faults and low-angle thrust faults influencing localization. Rocks east of the replaced units are hard, laminated sandstone and siltstone. Carbonate-bearing rocks are converted to calcsilicate rocks with light brown garnet; vesuvianite and scheelite are reported in mine waste dumps.
items.ore: Chalcopyrite, pyrrhotite, and minor sphalerite and galena in a gangue of calcite, garnet, and vesuvianite.
items.hostDescription: Limestone interbedded with dark calcareous phyllite.

history.event: Operated continuously; produced the major portion of the gold, silver, copper, and lead for the district.

Production resumed; Bullion Monarch Co. held a 50% interest in M.& B. Mining Co. By October 1980 about 1,000 tons of ore had been mined and stockpiled for delivery to Monarch's 200-tons-per-day flotation mill at Austin.

items.mineralsNote: 10 valid minerals per Mindat; vesuvianite additionally reported in mine waste dumps.
references: Ransome (1909). Notes on Some Mining Districts in Humboldt County, Nevada.
